name: Crepes Suzette
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat together eggs, flour, heavy cream, milk, and a pinch of salt until the mixture is smooth.
2. Melt one teaspoon of butter in a six-inch skillet.
3. When the butter foams, pour about one tablespoon of batter into the skillet.
4. Cook over moderately high heat, swirling the skillet so that the batter coats the bottom of the pan.
5. Cook for one minute, then check to ensure the bottom is browned.
6. Flip the crepe and cook the other side for about half a minute.
7. Continue making crepes this way until all of the batter is used.
8. Fold the cooked crepes into quarters and stack them on a plate covered with a tea towel.
9. Grate the rind of orange and lemon, then squeeze the juice from both fruits into a saucepan.
10. Add powdered sugar to the mixture in the saucepan and heat, stirring until smooth.
11. Pour the prepared sauce into a pitcher.
12. Melt butter in a chafing dish.
13. When the butter bubbles, add half of the liquors (kirsch, white curacao, maraschino) and ignite the flame.
14. Once the flame dies down, pour the sauce into the chafing dish and stir.
15. Add the cooked crepes to the dish, dipping and turning them to coat with the sauce.
16. Arrange the crepes in an overlapping layer.
17. Add the remaining liquors and ignite again.
18. Serve immediately once the flame dies down.
